channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: ICE arrests illegal Guatemalan migrant man facing multiple child rape charges in Massachusetts
item.description: An illegal immigrant from Guatemala was apprehended by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) earlier this month, who is facing multiple child rape charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man charged for strangling victim in Japan, cyberstalking from Utah
item.description: A Utah man has been indicted for his involvement in strangling and cyberstalking a victim in Japan — and he could face up to 10 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Eastern storm cuts power to tens of thousands as California braces for flooding
item.description: Storms dumped heavy snow and freezing rain on a swath of the U.S. East from Kentucky to the nation's capital, causing hundreds of traffic accidents, knocking out power in places and threatening to flood waterways as temperatures began rising Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JOHN RABY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New York state prison placed on lockdown after inmates take over, injure three corrections officers
item.description: A New York state prison was placed on lockdown after inmates took over and injured three guards, just days after staffing shortage concerns were raised.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Early look: The views and venues of the 2026 Winter Olympics
item.description: The 2026 Winter Games will bring the world's best athletes to Northern Italy, with Milan and Cortina as key host cities, marking the first time in 20 years that the games return to Italy.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Orange, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pilot killed in collision involving Mötley Crüe singer's plane identified
item.description: Joie Vitosky, 78, was certified as a flight engineer since 1978 and a pilot since 2019, according to FAA records.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Barker, NewsNation

item.title: White House says it has the right to punish AP reporters over Gulf naming dispute
item.description: The White House says news organizations that refuse to use President Donald Trump’s new name for the Gulf of Mexico are telling “lies.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former longtime Illinois legislative leader Michael Madigan is convicted in corruption trial
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — A Chicago Democrat who once set much of Illinois’ political agenda as the longest-serving legislative leader in U.S. history was convicted of conspiracy, bribery and wire fraud Wednesday in a split verdict in his high-profile corruption trial.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: MD, Baltimore mayor target Glock for selling pistols that can be converted into machine guns
item.description: The Maryland Attorney General and mayor of Baltimore City filed a lawsuit against Glock, Inc. for continuing to sell handguns that can be converted into machine guns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The relationship between the White House and its press corps is time-tested
item.description: This week, the White House barred Associated Press journalists from three media appearances by President Donald Trump — two of them in the Oval Office itself.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Navy crew members eject from their jet before it crashes off the San Diego coast
item.description: Authorities say two crew members ejected from a U.S. Navy jet before it crashed off the coast of San Diego and were rescued by a fishing boat.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on RFK Jr. to lead HHS
item.description: The 53-47 vote came as Democrats have been forcing Republicans to use up all debate time on President Trump’s most controversial nominees.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: When does a heartbeat start? South Carolina Supreme Court again takes up abortion issue
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — With a heartbeat abortion ban solidly in place in South Carolina, lawyers for the state and Planned Parenthood return to the state's highest court Wednesday to argue how restrictive the ban should be.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Want to send video of elephant pooping to your ex for V-day? Here's how
item.description: Want to "name a turd after a turd" for Valentine's Day? The Memphis Zoo has you covered by sending a video to your ex or your least favorite person.
item.dc:creator: Deja Davis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bryan Kohberger defense sounds alarm on unidentified blood at student murders home
item.description: University of Idaho student murder suspect Bryan Kohberger's defense is arguing that two blood samples from unidentified males at the crime scene could clear him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 14 bands and artists nominated for Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
item.description: Mariah Carey, Chubby Checker, Cyndi Lauper, OutKast and Phish are some the 2025 nominees for induction into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame, a list that also includes two sets of musical brothers who have had public feuds and recent reunions — the Black Crowes and Oasis.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Musk appears at White House defending DOGE's work but acknowledging mistakes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's most powerful adviser, Elon Musk, made a rare public appearance at the White House on Tuesday to defend the swift and extensive cuts he's pushing across the federal government while acknowledging there have been mistakes and will be more.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: In rare case, Georgia father jailed after his son was accused of a school shooting is granted bond
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — In a rare case of a parent being charged after a school shooting, a judge on Tuesday granted a $500,000 bond for the father of a 14-year-old boy accused of a deadly attack at a north Georgia high school.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Flu activity now highest in 15 years: See which states have it worst
item.description: Influenza cases continue to mount with the virus reaching activity levels in the U.S. not seen for the last 15 years, according to the Centers from Disease Control and Prevention.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Daily average of known gotaways at southern border plummets, down 93% from Biden administration highs
item.description: The average daily number of known gotaways at the southern border has dropped 93% since President Donald Trump returned to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watch: Musk joins Trump in Oval Office to tout DOGE
item.description: Despite Musk’s pledge to be “maximally transparent,” the White House on Tuesday fired the inspector general for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration appeals Maryland judge's ruling blocking birthright citizenship order
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration on Tuesday said it's appealing a Maryland federal judge's ruling blocking the president's executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship for people whose parents are not legally in the country.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Salman Rushdie testifies about his shock and pain as an attacker repeatedly stabbed him on stage
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — With a mix of humor and graphic detail, Salman Rushdie calmly told a jury Tuesday about the frenzied moments in August 2022 when a masked man rushed at him on a stage in western New York and repeatedly slashed him with a knife, leaving him with terrible injuries.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON and HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Canned tuna sold at major grocers recalled due to food poisoning risk
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - Several brands of canned tuna sold at major stores, including Trader Joe's, Costco, and H-E-B, have been recalled due to possible contamination. Tri-Union Seafoods, based in El Segundo, California, announced the recall last week.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: A pilot on a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer killed in Arizona aircraft collision
item.description: SCOTTSDALE, Ariz. (AP) — One of two pilots on a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil died after the aircraft veered off a runway in Arizona and hit a business jet, according to a Federal Aviation Administration incident report.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Survivors of child abuse at Illinois youth detention centers file more lawsuits in hopes of change
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Marcus Walker said the sexual abuse he experienced two decades ago as a teenager at an Illinois youth detention facility has haunted his life. After a staff member gave him drugs and sexually abused him, he joined a gang hoping it would protect him. It didn't.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Most US kids aren't gaining fast enough in reading and math. These schools are different
item.description: COMPTON, Calif. (AP) — Math is the subject sixth grader Harmoni Knight finds hardest, but that's changing. In-class tutors and “data chats” at her middle school in Compton, California, have made a dramatic difference, the 11-year-old said.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon pleads guilty and avoids jail time in border wall fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon pleaded guilty on Tuesday to defrauding donors to a private effort to build a wall on the U.S. southern border, ending a case the conservative strategist decried as a “political persecution.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Massachusetts top court rules Karen Read can be retried in her boyfriend's death
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The state’s top court ruled Tuesday that Karen Read can be retried on all the same charges in the death of her Boston police officer boyfriend, the latest twist in the long-running case that transfixed true crime fans nationwide.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Crush of Valentine's Day flowers: Nearly a billion go through this city to you
item.description: If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Roses are red, violets are blue, 940 million flowers are traveling (through Miami) to you
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers. In the run up to Feb. 14, agricultural specialists at Miami International Airport have processed about 940 million stems of cut flowers, according to U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs executive order restoring plastic straw use in government
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order directing the federal government to return to using plastic straws.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: Canned tuna sold at Walmart, Trader Joe's, Costco, others is recalled in multiple states
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has announced a recall on canned tuna products sold at several different retailers including Walmart, Trader Joe's, Kroger, and Costco.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer in fatal collision at Arizona airport
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Brother of Parkland shooter arrested for trespassing at 3 schools
item.description: Zachary Cruz, the 24-year-old brother of the Parkland school shooter, was arrested Feb. 4 for trespassing at multiple school properties, including Wilson Middle School, Wilson High School, and the Valley Career and Technical Center.
item.dc:creator: Ciara Sutherland, WFLA, NBC
